info.title: Woodpecker CI API
info.description:
Woodpecker is a simple, yet powerful CI/CD engine with great extensibility.
To get a personal access token (PAT) for authentication, please log in your Woodpecker server,
and go to you personal profile page, by clicking the user icon at the top right.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: for response pagination, page offset number

parameters.name: perPage
parameters.description: for response pagination, max items per page

post.description: Creates a new agent with a random token

requestBody.description: the agent's data (only 'name' and 'no_schedule' are read)

get.summary: Provide pipeline status information to the CCMenu tool
get.description:
CCMenu displays the pipeline status of projects on a CI server as an item in the Mac's menu bar.
More details on how to install, you can find at http://ccmenu.org/
The response format adheres to CCTray v1 Specification, https://cctray.org/v1/

get.summary: Get status of pipeline as SVG badge

get.description: Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug

get.summary: Get pprof stack traces that led to blocking on synchronization primitives
get.description: Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug

get.summary: Get the command line invocation of the current program
get.description: Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug

parameters.name: debug
parameters.description: Use debug=2 as a query parameter to export in the same format as an un-recovered panic

get.summary: Get pprof heap dump, a sampling of memory allocations of live objects
get.description: Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug

parameters.name: gc
parameters.description: You can specify gc=heap to run GC before taking the heap sample

get.description:
Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug
After you get the profile file, use the go tool pprof command to investigate the profile.

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: You can specify the duration in the seconds GET parameter.

get.summary: Get pprof program counters mapping to function names
get.description:
Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug
Looks up the program counters listed in the request,
responding with a table mapping program counters to function names.
The requested program counters can be provided via GET + query parameters,
or POST + body parameters. Program counters shall be space delimited.

post.summary: Get pprof program counters mapping to function names
post.description:
Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug
Looks up the program counters listed in the request,
responding with a table mapping program counters to function names.
The requested program counters can be provided via GET + query parameters,
or POST + body parameters. Program counters shall be space delimited.

get.summary: Get pprof stack traces that led to the creation of new OS threads
get.description: Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug

get.summary: Get a trace of execution of the current program
get.description:
Only available, when server was started with WOODPECKER_LOG_LEVEL=debug
After you get the profile file, use the go tool pprof command to investigate the profile.

get.description: Endpoint returns the server version and build information.

last_work.description: last time the agent did something, this value is used to determine if the agent is still doing work used by the autoscaler

org_id.description: OrgID is counted as unset if set to -1, this is done to ensure a new(Agent) still enforce the OrgID check by default

creator_id.description: TODO: drop with next major version

oauth_host.description: public url for oauth if different from url
public url for oauth if different from url

sender.description: uses reported user for webhooks and name of cron for cron pipelines

forge_remote_id.description: ForgeRemoteID is the unique identifier for the repository on the forge.

has_forge_name_conflict.description: HasForgeNameConflict is true if forge returned a repo with same name but different forge remote id

has_no_forge_repo.description: HasNoForgeRepo is true if repo only exist in the woodpecker store and not at the forge anymore

forge_remote_id.description: ForgeRemoteID is the unique identifier for the repository on the forge.

x-enum-comments.StatusError: error with the config / while parsing / some other system problem
x-enum-comments.StatusSkipped: skipped as per condition of current workflow failed/success state

x-enum-descriptions: skipped as per condition of current workflow failed/success state
error with the config / while parsing / some other system problem

admin.description:
Admin indicates the user is a system administrator.

NOTE: If the username is part of the WOODPECKER_ADMIN
environment variable, this value will be set to true on login.

avatar_url.description: the avatar url for this user.

email.description:
Email is the email address for this user.

required: true

id.description:
the id for this user.

required: true

login.description:
Login is the username for this user.

required: true

org_id.description: OrgID is the of the user as model.Org.

x-enum-comments.PipelineErrorTypeCompiler: some error with the config semantics
x-enum-comments.PipelineErrorTypeLinter: some error with the config syntax

some error with the config semantics

x-enum-comments.RequireApprovalAllEvents: require approval for all external events
x-enum-comments.RequireApprovalForks: require approval for PRs from forks (default)
